source: Verified specifications from manufacturer data. Neck thickness 21.59mm/24.13mm, joint angle 4 deg, headstock angle 17 deg confirmed from spec sheet 2026-03-29. Semi-hollow body (thinline) with laminated maple top/back/sides and solid maple center block.

standard.description: Gibson ES-335 — semi-hollow thinline electric. Laminated maple body with solid center block eliminates feedback while retaining acoustic resonance. The defining semi-hollow guitar, introduced 1958.

neck.depth_note: Neck-only depth (back of neck to underside of fretboard). Add fretboard thickness (~5mm) for total stack.
neck.note: ES-335 neck is noticeably chunkier than Explorer — 21.59mm at 1st vs Explorer 20.32mm, 24.13mm at 12th vs Explorer 21.59mm. Set neck with long tenon glued into the center block, not the hollow wings.

headstock.note: Standard Gibson open-book headstock at 17 deg — steeper than Explorer (14 deg).

body.center_block_note: Solid maple center block runs full length of body. Neck tenon glues into the center block.

comparison_to_explorer.note: Key differences from Gibson Explorer (Smart Guitar primary parent):
comparison_to_explorer.body_construction: ES-335 semi-hollow vs Explorer solid body
